30. Condamne les attaques dirigées contre des installations pétrolières et des sociétés  pétrolières  et  leurs  employés,  ainsi  que  toutes  hostilités  autour  de  ces installations,  et prie  instamment toutes  les  parties  d'assurer  la  sécurité  de l'infrastructure économique;
items_en: 19. Condemns in the strongest terms all attacks and threats against UNMISS personnel and United Nations facilities, as well as against IGAD personnel and facilities, stresses that such attacks may constitute violations of the Agreement on the Status of War Forces or War Crimes, requires all parties to respect the inviolability of United Nations premises and to refrain immediately from any violence against those gathered therein, reaffirms that the Interim Government of National Unity is bound by the provisions of the Agreement and further demands the immediate and safe release of the abducted and detained United Nations and associated personnel;

20. Condemns the clashes that took place in Malakal in February 2016 and the fighting in Juba in July 2016, and urges the United Nations to take into account the lessons learned in undertaking reforms within UNMISS in order to enhance its ability to carry out its mandate, in particular with regard to the protection of civilians, and to improve the Mission's chain of command, increase the effectiveness of its operations, enhance the safety and security of personnel and strengthen UNMISS' capacity to manage complex situations;
21. Reiterates its request to UNMISS to continue to take measures, as appropriate, to ensure the safety of its air operations in South Sudan and to report thereon to the Security Council;

22. Condemns in the strongest terms the attacks on humanitarian assistance and the looting of humanitarian relief, including food and medicine, as well as the looting of facilities such as hospitals and warehouses, and demands that the parties allow, in accordance with the applicable provisions of international law and the guiding principles of the United Nations on emergency humanitarian assistance, namely, humanity, impartiality, neutrality and independence, prompt, safe and free access to relief personnel, equipment and supplies, and the expeditious delivery of humanitarian assistance to all those in need throughout South Sudan, in particular internally displaced persons and refugees, and stresses that any return must be voluntary, informed, dignified and secure, and that the same applies to any other durable solution concerning internally displaced persons and refugees;

23. Demands that all parties immediately cease all forms of violence, violations of and violations of human rights and violations of international humanitarian law, including rape and other forms of sexual and gender-based violence, and that perpetrators of such acts be held accountable in order to break the current cycle of impunity;

24. Condemns all violations of applicable international law, including international humanitarian law, as well as human rights violations and abuses committed by all parties to the conflict, in particular against children, and urges the parties to implement the conclusions and commitments contained in paragraph 25 of its resolution 2252 (2015) in order to end violations and abuses committed against children, including the immediate release of all children within their ranks;

25. Urges SPLA, SPLA in the opposition and other armed groups to prevent further acts of sexual violence, urges the Provisional Government of National Unity and SPLA in the opposition to implement the commitments and plans of action they have adopted jointly and unilaterally for the prevention of conflict-related sexual violence, which emphasize the prevention, accountability and strengthening of victim assistance, and urges SPLA leaders to issue explicit orders on the prevention of conflict-related sexual violence, and calls upon the Interim Government of National Unity to take concrete measures to ensure that its forces committing sexual violence are held accountable;

26. Stresses that truth-seeking and reconciliation are essential for peace in South Sudan, and in this regard stresses that the Truth, Reconciliation and Peace Commission has a key role to play in peacebuilding in South Sudan, as outlined in the Agreement, by leading efforts to achieve national cohesion and promote peace, national reconciliation and calm;

27. Takes note of the steps taken by the African Union towards the establishment of the Joint Tribunal for South Sudan as provided for in chapter V of the Agreement and the action taken to date by the United Nations, welcomes the formal request by the African Union to the Organization to provide technical assistance for the establishment of the Joint Tribunal, and requests the Secretary-General to continue to provide technical assistance to the African Union Commission and the Interim Government of National Union for the establishment of the Joint Tribunal for South Sudan and the implementation of other parts of chapter V of the Agreement, including the establishment of the Truth, Reconciliation and Appreciation Commission;

28. Calls upon the Government of South Sudan to carry out expeditiously and transparently investigations into allegations of human rights violations and violations, in compliance with its international obligations, and invites the Government to publish the reports of such investigations, including the recent investigation into the events at the Terrain hotel complex;

29. Also calls upon the Government of South Sudan, while taking note of chapter V, paragraph 3.2.2, of the Agreement, to hold accountable all those responsible for human rights violations and violations of international humanitarian law, to ensure equal protection of the law and equal access to justice for all victims of sexual violence, and to ensure equal respect for the rights of women and girls in these proceedings, and noting that comprehensive transitional justice measures, including measures to ensure accountability, truth-seeking and reparation, are fundamental to the achievement of calm and reconciliation;

30. Condemns the attacks on oil installations and oil companies and their employees, as well as all hostilities surrounding such installations, and urges all parties to ensure the security of economic infrastructure;

text_en: Reports

items_fr: 31. Prie le  Secrétaire  général  de  lui  fournir,  dans  un  délai  de  30  jours,  des informations  détaillées  sur  la  constitution  des  forces,  la  restructuration  de  la  force de  la  MINUSS,  le  soutien  logistique,  les  éléments  habilitants  et  le  personnel  civil chargé  d'appliquer  le  mandat,  et  de  lui  indiquer  si  le  Gouvernement  provisoire d'union  nationale  consent  toujours,  en  principe,  au  déploiement  de  la  force  de protection régionale et n'a pas créé d'obstacles politiques ou opérationnels à la mise en place effective de la force ni empêché la MINUSS de s'acquitter de son mandat, et prie également le Secrétaire général d'examiner les besoins sur le terrain et de lui fournir  une  évaluation  actualisée  des  opérations,  du  déploiement  et  des  besoins futurs de la force de protection régionale, ainsi que de toutes les entraves politiques ou  opérationnelles  à  la  mise  en  place  effective  de  la  force  et  manœuvres d'obstruction destinées à empêcher la MINUSS de s'acquitter de son mandat, dans les  30  jours  suivant  l'adoption  de  la présente  résolution  et tous  les  30 jours  par  la suite;

32. Prie le  Secrétaire  général de lui faire rapport sur l'exécution du  mandat de la MINUSS, y compris la force de protection régionale, ainsi que sur les progrès accomplis  dans  la  mise  en  œuvre  de  la  Politique  de  diligence  voulue  visée  au paragraphe 17 ci-dessus, et de lui présenter des informations à jour sur la  manière dont  la  Mission  s'acquitte  de  ses  tâches  de  protection  des  civils,  notamment,  mais pas  exclusivement,  sur  les  nouvelles  zones  de  patrouille  et  les  déploiements systématiques  visés  au  paragraphe  15  ci-dessus,  et  sur  la  prise  en  compte  de  la problématique  hommes-femmes  dans  tous  les  aspects  du  mandat  de  la  Mission, comme le prévoit le paragraphe 14 ci-dessus, et de faire des recommandations sur les  mesures  à  prendre  pour  adapter  la  Mission  à  la  situation  sur  le  terrain  et  lui permettre  de  s'acquitter  plus  efficacement  de  son  mandat,  dans  un  unique  rapport écrit détaillé devant être soumis dans les 90 jours suivant l'adoption de la présente résolution, puis tous les 90 jours par la suite;

33. Rappelle le paragraphe 6 de sa résolution 2304 (2016), prie le Secrétaire général  de  poursuivre  ses  consultations  avec  les  pays  qui  fournissent  des contingents et du personnel de police en vue de renforcer la sûreté et la sécurité du personnel  de  la  MINUSS  afin  que  celle-ci  puisse  s'acquitter  efficacement  de  son mandat dans des conditions de sécurité complexes, et prie  également le  Secrétaire général de lui rendre compte, dans le cadre de ses rapports périodiques, des mesures prises  pour  renforcer  la  sûreté  et  la  sécurité  du  personnel  des  Nations  Unies,  ainsi que  des  réformes  visant  à  permettre  à  la  MINUSS  de  mieux  s'acquitter  de  son mandat, notamment l'amélioration de la chaîne de commandement, l'accroissement de l'efficacité des opérations et le renforcement de l'aptitude de la Mission à gérer les situations complexes, comme demandées au paragraphe 18 ci-dessus;

34. Prie le  Secrétaire  général  de  présenter,  dans  les  six  mois  suivant l'adoption de la présente résolution, un bilan des progrès accomplis par les parties en ce qui concerne la cessation des hostilités, la reprise du dialogue et l'instauration d'une  représentation  inclusive  au  sein  du  Gouvernement,  ainsi  que  de  faire  des recommandations concernant les ajustements à apporter au mandat de la MINUSS;

35. Prie le  Secrétaire  général  de  lui  rendre  compte,  dans  ses  rapports trimestriels,  de  l'assistance  technique  fournie  en  application  du  paragraphe  25  cidessus, invite l'Union  africaine  à  communiquer  au  Secrétaire  général,  aux  fins  de l'établissement  de  son  rapport,  des  informations  sur  les  progrès  accomplis  dans  la
items_en: 31. Requests the Secretary-General to provide, within 30 days, detailed information on the formation of the force, the restructuring of the UNMISS force, logistical support, enabling elements and civilian personnel responsible for the implementation of the mandate, and to indicate whether the Interim Government of National Unity continues to consent, in principle, to the deployment of the regional protection force and has not created political or operational obstacles to the effective establishment of the force or prevented UNMISS from carrying out its mandate, and also requests the Secretary-General to review the requirements in the field and provide an updated assessment of the operations, deployment and future needs of the regional protection force, as well as any political or operational impediments to the effective implementation of the force and obstructions to UNMISS' ability to fulfil its mandate, within 30 days of the adoption of this resolution and every 30 days thereafter;

32. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly on the implementation of the mandate of UNMISS, including the regional protection force, as well as on the progress made in the implementation of the Due Diligence Policy referred to in paragraph 17 above, and to provide updated information on the Mission's performance of its civilian protection tasks, including, but not limited to, the new patrol areas and systematic deployments referred to in paragraph 15 above, and on gender mainstreaming in all aspects of the Mission's mandate, as provided for in paragraph 14 above, and to make recommendations on measures to adapt the Mission to the situation on the ground and to enable it to fulfil its mandate more effectively, in a single detailed written report to be submitted within 90 days of the adoption of the present resolution, and every 90 days thereafter;

33. Recalls paragraph 6 of its resolution 2304 (2016), requests the Secretary-General to continue his consultations with troop- and police-contributing countries with a view to enhancing the safety and security of UNMISS personnel in order to enable it to carry out its mandate effectively in complex security conditions, and also requests the Secretary-General to report, in his periodic reports, on measures taken to enhance the safety and security of United Nations personnel, as well as on reforms to enable UNMISS to better fulfil its mandate, including improving the chain of command, enhancing the effectiveness of operations and strengthening the Mission's capacity to manage complex situations, as requested in paragraph 18 above;

34. Requests the Secretary-General to report, within six months of the adoption of the present resolution, on the progress made by the parties with regard to the cessation of hostilities, the resumption of dialogue and the establishment of inclusive representation in the Government, and to make recommendations on adjustments to the mandate of UNMISS;

35. Requests the Secretary-General to report, in his quarterly reports, on the technical assistance provided in accordance with paragraph 25 above, invites the African Union to provide the Secretary-General with information on progress made in the preparation of his report on the implementation of the resolution.

text_fr: mise  en  place  du  Tribunal  mixte  pour  le  Soudan  du  Sud,  et déclare son  intention d'évaluer, lorsqu'il recevra les rapports du Secrétaire général, le travail accompli en vue de l'établissement du Tribunal mixte, conformément aux normes internationales;
text_en: Establishment of the Joint Tribunal for South Sudan, and expresses its intention to assess, upon receipt of the reports of the Secretary-General, the work done towards the establishment of the Joint Tribunal in accordance with international standards;

items_fr: 36. Décide de rester saisi de la question.
items_en: 36. Decides to remain seized of the matter.
